steps.text: Obtain approval from the director of the CC/CO/NC(s) for CDC sponsorship of conferences that include funding.
Follow financial support procedures as described in Section 4H (1) or (2).
Obtain approval from the director of the CC/CO/NC(s) for CDC sponsorship of conferences that do not include funding.
Ensure sponsorship is consistent with CDC's policy on Guidance for Collaboration with the Private Sector and HHS OGC, Ethics Division Co-Sponsorship Guidance.
Ensure that any conference sponsored by CDC in domestic facilities is fully accessible to the public as required by ADAAG.
Allow conference organizer(s) to use CDC's name only in factual publicity and ensure compliance with promotional material restrictions.
Obtain approval or disapproval for requests for technical assistance, participation in, or attending the conference by CDC staff members according to procedures determined by the employee's CC/CO/NC.
Adhere to CDC Guiding Principles for the Conduct of Scientific Conferences when applicable.
Ensure that CDC-sponsored conferences/meetings are managed in a transparent, objective, and unbiased manner.
Use existing conference grant or contract mechanisms or develop written guidelines and processes in accordance with existing procurement and ethics laws, rules and policies.

Provide CDC with documentation of a plan with a timeline for substantive involvement in, and development of, the content of the meeting, including standards that ensure scientific and public health merit.
Provide a written process of how presentations or abstracts are to be reviewed and criteria that will be used for selecting presentations.
Agree to abide by CDC Guiding Principles for the Conduct of Scientific Conferences, when applicable.
Establish a written process for approving conference funding.
Solicit proposals for conference funding.
Define elements required in a proposal.
Review and approve proposals by the CC/CO/NC.
Ensure review by a Center or Division level appointed committee composed of 3 staff members or peers per meeting.
Obtain final approval from the CC/CO/NC Director.
The CC/CO/NC Director, or his/her designee, is responsible for reviewing and approving or disapproving all requests submitted to the CC/CO/NC.
Provide a written response to the conference organizer(s) indicating disapproval of the request if the CC/CO/NC or the OD, CDC, does not wish to sponsor the conference.
Provide a written response to the conference organizer(s) indicating tentative approval if the CC/CO/NC is interested in sponsoring the conference.
Include specific language in the response regarding CDC's active participation in the conference development and approval.
After reviewing the full final conference agenda and other conference information, provide a written response to the conference organizer(s) indicating final approval or disapproval of sponsorship.
Complete a co-sponsorship agreement with the non-federal co-sponsor once final approval is provided.
Assure that the proposed meeting facilities comply with ADAAG prior to approving any request or issuing any obligation documents.
Obtain written certification from management of the proposed meeting facility stating that facilities, goods and services are fully ADA compliant.
Ensure that the CC/CO/NC completes the Accessible Meetings Checklist and keeps it on file with other conference documentation.
Maintain a copy of all written correspondence regarding conferences that CDC is asked to sponsor.
Include documentation required as per Section 4H.
Account fully and accurately for each party’s programmatic and financial responsibilities and activities.
Describe separately any discrete portion of an event that will be exclusively sponsored by CDC or exclusively sponsored by a non-Federal entity.
Make agreements and records concerning the amounts, sources, and uses of funds available to the public upon request.
Assign a specific person the responsibility for maintaining these records.
Submit a quarterly line listing of all conferences sponsored or disapproved to the OD, CDC.
Ensure the OD maintains records of these listings and makes them available to the CC/CO/NCs upon request.
Inform private partners about the collaborative relationship being made a matter of public record.
Work out how the public record will occur, especially if implied endorsements are likely.
Maintain a process of openness and fairness in developing collaborations with partners.
Establish a formal review and advisory process within each coordinating center for examining potential collaborations.
Receive orientation and guidance from the CDC Ethics Officer regarding collaboration principles.

constraints.text: Any conference sponsored by CDC must meet the accessibility requirements of the Americans with Disabilities Act Accessibility Guidelines (ADAAG).
Non-federal sponsors must clear all promotional or publicity materials for the event with CDC.
All conferences sponsored by CDC shall be held in facilities that are fully accessible to the public as required by American with Disabilities Act Accessibility Guidelines (ADAAG).
CDC shall not co-sponsor an event with an entity that will not make information concerning funding publicly available.
No direct endorsement of a product or service is permitted by the agency.
